title: Lazy Evaluation: Computation on Demand
overview: Delaying the evaluation of an expression until its value is actually required by the program.
historical_context: Derived from the Lambda Calculus and popularized by Haskell as a way to handle infinite data structures and optimize execution.
mental_model: Think of a recipe where you only chop the vegetables when you are actually ready to put them in the pan.
tradeoffs: Difficult to predict memory usage and timing; can lead to "space leaks."
legacy: A defining feature of Haskell and an optional optimization in many other languages (e.g., Python generators, Scala lazy vals).
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Analyze how lazy evaluation enables infinite lists in Haskell.
Explain the "thunk" mechanism used to implement laziness.
